items.title: 'Plate to paddock': How a festival's waste became a farmer's treasure

children.content: Tonnes of organic waste from the National Folk Festival are being turned into fertiliser on a farm outside Canberra in a circular economy project delivering more benefits than just waste reduction.

A man ina green shirt stands on his farm.
A man ina green shirt stands on his farm.

items.title: Alpine ash endangered listing 'will do nothing to protect them'

children.content: Scientists hope an endangered listing may help preserve alpine ash forests, but the forestry industry is raising concerns that it could result in further decline.

image.altText: Aerial view of burnt pine forests.

items.title: 'Into the hands of Trump': Dan Duggan's bid to avoid extradition fails

children.content: Former United States Marine Corps pilot Dan Duggan’s Federal Court bid to avoid extradition from Australia is dismissed.

image.altText: Former US military pilot Daniel Edmund Duggan seen as a pilot in Tasmania

items.title: Two men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Irma Palasics

children.content: Two Melbourne men are found guilty of the manslaughter of Canberra woman Irma Palasics during a violent home invasion in 1999.

gallery.altText: Irma Palasics smiles at the camera, she has short curly hair and wears a pink knit vest.
A man stands outside a court house speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: A lucky chat, then a lifeline for men with prostate cancer

children.content: Michael Platts panicked when he was diagnosed with prostate cancer. Now he wants to help others break through the stigma and silence surrounding the condition.

image.altText: A farmer in his 60s in a blue shirt stands in a paddock looking into the camera.

items.title: 'Your courage does not end': Prince Harry addresses room of veterans in Canberra

children.content: The Duke of Sussex flew solo to the nation's capital and addressed an Invictus Australia event at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: Prince Harry attends an Invictus Australia reception at the Aust

items.title: Pressure increases on CIT CEO to stand down

children.content: An independent MLA is calling for the head of CIT to stand down, claiming statutory declarations show she "clearly misled" the board prior to her appointment, despite knowing about misconduct findings.

image.altText: Man with a beard wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Chinese device found in strategic waters near Bali identified

children.content: A large torpedo-shaped device discovered near an island in a strategically important strait between Bali and Lombok has been identified by defence analysts as a Chinese undersea monitoring system.

gallery.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

items.title: Europe was a word British PMs dared not say. Then Donald Trump returned

children.content: Keir Starmer was never a fan of Brexit. Now, under the cover of the war in Iran and a fractured US relationship, he's looking to Europe to return Britain to the fold.

image.altText: Keir Starmer speaks in the House of Commons

avatar.alt: Brett Worthington stands in a courtyard inside parliament house

items.title: Young people leaving Sydney 'en mass' over housing affordability

children.content: Productivity Commissioner Danielle Wood says Australia will not meet the federal government's 1.2 million new home target, as residential construction rates fall.

image.altText: A photo of a scaffold for a house being built in Brisbane

items.title: Bank warns of significant further surge in inflation as oil price spikes

children.content: The Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) expects Australian inflation to surge to 6 per cent, while adding another RBA interest rate hike to its forecast. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: a petrol pump

items.title: Mayor fears hidden cameras may have been in town hall change rooms for years

children.content: The Horsham mayor is worried that hidden cameras reportedly found in the town hall theatre change rooms could have been there for years.

image.altText: Inside Horsham Town Hall foyer, turquoise wall in background, chairs and tables in foreground

items.title: Mother of 11 who 'never thought we'd be homeless' pleads for help

children.content: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

items.title: Sydney childcare worker hit with 129 new child abuse charges

children.content: A Sydney childcare worker accused of using children to make abuse material has been charged with 129 new offences.

image.altText: A young child stacks colourful Duplo blocks at a childcare centre. Other children stand next to them.

items.title: Missing votes counted in key SA seat see One Nation margin increase

children.content: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

image.altText: One Nation's Chantelle Thomas at a media conference.

items.title: Some Australians fear lockdowns. Economists say stagflation is the bigger risk

children.content: As fuel costs surge and supply fears grow, businesses and households say the economic shock from the war in the Middle East is already hitting home.

image.altText: A price board displaying petrol for more than $2.47 per litre.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

items.title: Why experts urge not to grow these popular invasive plants in your home

children.content: Agapanthus, morning glory or gazanias might be easy-growing plants in your home but their invasive nature can damage your garden and the environment. Here's what experts recommend.

items.title: How mobile phone cameras have helped unearth a mysterious kingdom

children.content: An exhibition bridges the gap between art and science by inviting visitors to imagine the world through the senses of other species and consider our own fragile relationship with nature.

image.altText: A cluster of cup-shaped mushrooms cling to a damp fallen branch amonst feathery ferns.

items.title: Australia is on the verge of aged care AI boom

children.content: Artificial intelligence-enabled robot companions, personal behaviour monitors and pain management apps could soon be used more widely within the sector.

image.altText: Andromeda's Abi robot

items.title: What we know about the 'small fire' that became an 'unprecedented' Geelong refinery blaze

children.content: The "unprecedented" fire at one of the two remaining oil refineries in Australia took more than 12 hours to extinguish. Here's what we know about how it unfolded.

articles.title: Queensland government in discussions for new oil refinery

children.content: As the fuel crisis continues, a proposal for an $11 billion new oil refinery in central Queensland has been put forward.

image.altText: A close up of the exhaust pipes at a petrol refinery.

articles.title: As Geelong's refinery burned, locals say its owner did not update them

children.content: Residents near a large oil refinery fire have told a community meeting that initial information from emergency services was misleading and there was a lack of updates.

image.altText: Signs at the Viva Energy facility in Geelong read 'Refinery under alarm'.

items.title: Migration debate deserves less politicking from the Liberals

children.content: The Liberals need to resolve the internal ambiguities they have on big policy questions when it comes to migration.

image.altText: Angus Taylor stands behind Jonno Duniam.

items.title: Trump repeats he is 'not happy with Australia' as Hormuz crisis continues

children.content: Donald Trump again accuses Australia of refusing to help the US in the Strait of Hormuz, but the Australian government maintains there has been no formal request from the US.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands outside the White House as reporters hold microphones.

items.title: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi terror attack survivors on Sydney leg of tour

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex spoke with lifeguards who responded to the December 14 Bondi Beach terror attack and survivors.

items.title: St Kilda announces appeal against Collard ban for homophobic slur

children.content: St Kilda is appealing the nine-match ban given to Lance Collard for uttering a homophobic comment, saying the young forward maintains his innocence.

image.altText: AFL player running with the ball, preparing to kick, during a warmup

items.title: Australian billionaire Andrew Forrest battles Meta over fake ads

children.content: Australian billionaire mining magnate Andrew Forrest is asking a US federal court in Silicon Valley to hold Meta accountable for scam ads using his likeness without permission.

image.altText: A side-on photo of a man wearing a blue suit, white shirt and yellow tie gesturing with his right hand while speaking on stage.

items.title: Elderly couple beg council to let them stay in Gold Coast caravan park

children.content: Time is running out for residents facing eviction from Kirra Beach Tourist Park. In 2018, the council told them they needed to move out by 2028 to make way for tourists, but with rising rent and property prices, they say they cannot afford to live anywhere else.

image.altText: man and woman with dog sitting on lap

items.title: Former Arsenal, Juventus goalkeeper killed after car hit by train

children.content: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger, who played for Arsenal and a string of Italian clubs, died on Thursday at the age of 48 after the car he was driving was hit by a train at a crossing near Salzburg.

image.altText: Car and train crash. Train into side of car, resting on railway

items.title: Iranian footballers granted asylum in Australia 'overwhelmed' by support

children.content: Two Iranian footballers given refuge in Australia after the AFC Women's Asian Cup say they are focused on their health, safety and rebuilding their lives.

items.title: Coca-Cola flags it could pull out of Qld recycling scheme

children.content: The Queensland government recently introduced proposed laws to overhaul the governance of the scheme, while also ruling out an increase to the 10-cent refund amount.

image.altText: Coke cans at a WA containers for change depot

items.title: Back on Earth, Artemis II crew still finding their footing

children.content: The astronauts say their lunar flyby puts NASA in a much better position for a crewed Moon landing in two years and an eventual Moon base.

items.title: Bank robbers hold 25 people hostage in Italy then escape into 'sewers'

children.content: The group took customers and employees hostage before fleeing with "the contents of dozens of safe deposit boxes", according to police.

image.altText: a smashed window at a bank building in naples, italy after an armed robbery took place

items.title: 'Mums and dads have been misled': AFL under fire over headgear

children.content: An AFL-endorsed helmet company has backtracked on its claims of "superior protection against concussion" and the league is now also facing a complaint lodged with the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission on the matter.

image.altText: A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: In charts: A look at Australia's record spending on defence

children.content: The Defence Department reveals where the money will be going as Australia plans to spend a record $425 billion over the next decade.
